name: Candy
description: Chocolate bars, hard candies, gummies, and the sugary staples of every corner store.

questions.q: M&M's, Snickers, and Milky Way are all candies made by which company?

source.notes: Mars is a family-owned private company.

questions.q: Which candy bar's slogan is 'Sometimes you feel like a nut, sometimes you don't'?

source.notes: Mounds has no nut; Almond Joy has an almond.

questions.q: Which hard round candy is shaped like a life preserver with a hole in the middle?

source.notes: Created 1912 by chocolate maker Clarence Crane.

questions.q: Which German company makes the original gummy bears (started 1922)?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: fixed typo 'starzed' -> 'started'. Name from founder Hans Riegel, Bonn.

questions.q: Reese's Peanut Butter Cups are made by which company (part of a giant chocolate conglomerate)?

source.notes: Hershey acquired Reese's in 1963.

questions.q: Which famous 1982 blockbuster created a huge demand spike for Reese's Pieces?

source.notes: Mars famously turned down the product placement for M&M's.

questions.q: Which candy is made of sugar spun into fine threads — often bought at fairs on a cone?

source.notes: Called 'candyfloss' in the UK.

questions.q: Which chocolate bar includes nougat, caramel, peanuts, and milk chocolate — arguably the world's best-selling bar?

source.notes: Launched 1930; named for a horse.

questions.q: Kit Kat bars are famously associated with which slogan?

source.notes: 'Gimme a break, gimme a break, break me off a piece of that Kit Kat bar.'

questions.q: Which colorful shell-covered candy uses the slogan 'Melts in your mouth, not in your hand'?

source.notes: Introduced 1941.

questions.q: Skittles adopts which slogan, inviting you to taste a color?

source.notes: Introduced 1994.

questions.q: Tootsie Rolls famously survived which 20th-century war trip, earning a military reputation?

source.notes: Air-dropped to Marines at Chosin in winter 1950; 'Tootsie Rolls' was also a code word for ammunition.

questions.q: Which Japanese biscuit-based chocolate stick comes in a slim red box?

source.notes: Launched 1966 by Ezaki Glico.

questions.q: Which Swedish-style gummy fish is a chewy red favorite at movie theaters?

source.notes: Originally Malaco's 'pastellfiskar.'

questions.q: Which classic British chocolate brand is owned by Mondelez and famous for its purple packaging?

source.notes: Founded 1824 in Birmingham.

questions.q: Which classic 1-2-3 bar from Nestlé combines rice-crisp texture with milk chocolate?

source.notes: Nestlé Crunch; Krackel is Hershey's.

questions.q: Which fiery cinnamon hard candy turns your mouth red with heat?

source.notes: All three are classic cinnamon candies.

questions.q: Which candy takes the shape of a corn kernel, usually tri-colored, and is synonymous with Halloween?

source.notes: Dates back to the 1880s.

questions.q: Before rebranding, Starburst candies were marketed under which name in the U.S.?

source.notes: Launched in the UK in 1960 as Opal Fruits; renamed Starburst in 1998 in UK.

questions.q: Which enduring chocolate bar originated in 1894 as the first mass-produced chocolate bar in America?

source.notes: Milton Hershey started with caramels, moved to chocolate in 1894.

questions.q: Which Swiss-founded brand makes the small triangular-shaped Toblerone chocolate bar?

source.notes: Created 1908 by Theodor Tobler; Mondelez now owns the brand.

questions.q: Which sour-gummy candy brand features 'mini-Swedish-style kids' that are sour then sweet?

source.notes: Originally 'Mars-Men' in the 1970s, rebranded in 1985.
